dialogues.text_en: The first caravan is due to depart shortly, followed by the rest at regular intervals. My family and I─including my stubborn old father Homei─will be traveling in the lead carriage together with young Koharu, whom I believe you've already met.
Though I understand the road to Ul'dah is regularly patrolled by your Brass Blades, it would give us great comfort if you would agree to accompany us on our journey...
Once again, I thank you for your kindness. When you are ready to leave, please inform our coachman.
A giant bird that one rides as if it were a horse? What a curious creature...
Eager to be off, are ye? Aye, well, I was meanin' to have a word with ye about that. Runner from the Blades says we might have a problem.
A fierce-lookin' beastie's been sighted south of the bridge to Hammerlea. Might be as someone's eyes are playin' tricks on 'em, o' course, but it don't pay to gamble in this business.

Now I know ye can handle yerself in a fight, but this lot here's another story. So I was thinkin' ye might go on ahead an' have a quick look around─make sure the area's safe, like. An' if ye come across anythin' dangerous, ye know what to do.

When yer satisfied the way's clear, meet us outside Horizon. The Blades tell me the road there's safe, so I reckon we can make it that far on our own─assumin' the selfsame bastards don't try to rob us, that is.
You go on ahead, son/miss, an' meet us in Horizon when yer satisfied our beastie problem's solved. We'll be on our way soon enough.
These reports of a great beast on the road to Ul'dah are most troubling...
Fascinating creatures... Whence did they come?
Ugh, Grandfather is so lucky. These─ hurk !─horsebirds smell terrible!
Ohh, it's just so... fluffy !
I must say, that was a surprisingly pleasant ride! Mayhap the journey to Revenant's Toll will not be so bad after all.
It pleases me to see you well. Come, let the coachman know we can continue onward to Ul'dah.
Which do you think would win in a race? A horse or a horsebird?
They have wings, so does that mean they can fly? And does that mean we can fly if we ride one!?
So there was a beastie! “Was” bein' the operative word, eh!? Hah hah!
Well fought, sir / madam , well fought! Ah, if only I were in better health, I should have been proud to stand at your side!
Pay my son no mind. He forgets that his responsibilities preclude acts of derring-do.
Hmph. Be that as it may, I cannot help but envy you. Had we been able to call upon heroes of your strength and skill in Doma, things would have been very different.
<sigh> But we cannot change the past. Those who fought─and fell─are forever lost to us.
Lost but not forgotten─Lady Yugiri's kinsmen least of all. They fought fiercest, though they knew full well it was in vain.
She had every right to walk away, yet she chose to stay. Food, shelter, the promise of a better life─all this and more we owe to her. How does one even begin to repay such a debt?
In kind, Father. To she who has given everything, we shall give our all.
Whatever work there is to be done we shall do without question. Ditch digging, bricklaying, it matters not─Revenant's Toll will grow and prosper by our hands.
Hey, Mister / Miss Forename ! Koharu and I want to know why you became an adventurer!
I knew it! That's what I told Koharu, but she didn't believe me.
Glory? Ah, like bringing honor to your family, you mean!? Yes, Father always goes on about that.
Money, eh? Yes, I want money, too. I could do a lot of things if I had some─well, any ...
So...you like roses? I think I understand... I mean, they're nice and everything... But don't they grow in lots of places?
It's my turn now! Excuse me, Mister / Miss Forename ! I've got a question! What's Revenant's Toll like? Is it pretty?
You mean...there are lots of people there who are just as strong as you!? Oh, I can't wait!
So...there are lots of rocks and things? It sounds a bit scary...but you'll be there too, won't you?
Oh, so you live there with your friends! That means you'll always be there to protect us!
Ha! You can't fool me! No one would ever live that close to a castrum. Liar, liar, breeches on fire!
Enough, Koharu! It's my turn again! Mister / Miss Forename ─when I'm older, I want to be strong like you. What do I have to do?
Of course! To be a better adventurer, you need to go adventuring !
Like that thing you fought earlier, you mean? Right, right!
Aha! So my father was right! How do you do it, then? Come on, tell me! And don't leave anything out!
Rowena? Is she nice? Are her weapons cheap? I don't have any money at the moment, but my father might. I'll have to ask him.
All right, all right─ That's enough questions for Mister / Miss Forename .
That's not fair! Why does Yozan get to ask two questions? I want to know about adventuring just as much as he does...
Just one more─please! Do you think I could be an adventurer like you one day?
Really!? Then I will! Thanks for the advice, Mister / Miss Forename . I won't forget it!
I'm going to be an adventurer too!
When we get to Revenant's Toll, let's make an Adventurers' Guild for Domans!
We'll protect our friends and punish the wicked─just like Mister / Miss Forename and Lady Yugiri!
Yeah! Just like Mister / Miss Forename and Lady Yugiri!
We can talk more about it in the carriage on the way. Will you be joining us?
Would you look at that...
It will be good to sleep in a proper bed for a change, even if it's only for a night.
I bet they could keep out an entire legion with those walls!
How many steps are there? A hundred? I'm going to count!
Mind lettin' Master Alphinaud know we're here? I need to stay with the birds.
I pray they do not grow too accustomed to this treatment. It is a welcome respite, to be sure, but a far cry from what we can expect in Revenant's Toll.
We welcome your return, Master / Mistress Surname .
Has the first group arrived already? Wonderful! Pray inform Master Alphinaud at once.
Ah, Forename . I take it the first group has arrived? Excellent.
Hm? Is there aught else? No, no. I have matters well in hand here. You and Lady Yugiri should travel to Revenant's Toll forthwith.
